description_long: This object is a roughly hewn stone block with an irregular, polygonal shape and faceted surfaces. Its exterior is predominantly a medium gray, with a distinct darker section along one edge, suggesting either a natural mineral variation or partial soiling. The block has sharp, angular corners and flat planes, giving it a geometric yet natural appearance. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, with no visible engravings or markings. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a substantial barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a street or park. The block is solid and heavy, likely composed entirely of stone.
description: A large, irregularly shaped gray stone block with faceted surfaces and a darker edge, appearing solid and heavy.
description_view_0: This view highlights the block's angular front face and the prominent dark section along the bottom edge.
description_view_1: From this angle, the block's irregular top surface and the extension of the dark edge along the side are visible.
description_view_2: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the block, with the dark section less visible.
description_view_3: The block appears more polygonal and uniform from this side, with the lighter gray color dominating.
attribute_installation_method: set in sand or directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; surfaces appear clean with minor discoloration along one edge.
